title: Technical Practice Node Schema
description: Schema for representing concepts that constitute technical practice (Philosophia Artium Technicarum et Operis), enabling modeling of agentic operations, artifacts, processes, and their interrelations.

name.description: Canonical name of the technical concept.

layer.description: Macro layer representing the primary operational stratum in which the technical concept functions. Problem Space captures requirements, purposes, constraints, and desired states that initiate technical practice. Capability Space captures agents, competencies, techniques, resources, and infrastructural capacities enabling intervention. Execution Space captures situated acts, mechanisms, operative techniques, processes, and real-time transformations performed on reality. Artifact Space captures blueprints, specifications, constitutive components, production objects, architectures, and functional structures generated or manipulated through technical practice. Governance Space captures standards, principles, security regimes, institutions, verification systems, and regulatory structures that constrain, validate, and coordinate technical operation. Evolutionary Space captures technical research, obsolescence, historical progression, innovation trajectories, and long-term transformation of technical systems over time.

category.description: Ontological classification of the node within the technical practice framework.

long_description.description: Detailed explanatory description of the element.

type_hierarchy.description: Ordered chain of parent and ancestor classes to which the element belongs.

interface_specification.description: External-facing contract describing purpose, function, inputs, outputs, and interaction surfaces.

operation.description: Description of how the element behaves during execution or use.

functional_role_set.description: The set of functions, responsibilities, or effects the element performs within a larger system.

implementation_specification.description: Internal realization of the element, including structure, architecture, composition, and operational mechanisms.

scope_specification.description: Conditions under which the element applies, including domain, operating environment, applicability boundaries, and performance characteristics.

limitation_set.description: Physical, logical, economic, organizational, regulatory, or environmental constraints limiting the element.

lifecycle.description: How the element is created, configured, deployed, operated, maintained, evolved, and retired.

failure_mode_specification.description: Ways in which the element can malfunction, degrade, violate specifications, or cease fulfilling its intended function.

resource_use.description: Matter, energy, information, labor, time, capital, or infrastructure consumed by the element.

relationship_set.description: Structured set of relations connecting the element to other technical elements.

evolution_history.description: Historical development, versions, predecessors, successors, and major changes over time.

particular_characterization.description: Category-specific attributes not covered by the generic schema.

categoryAmbiguity.description: Captures alternative plausible category classifications when the node spans multiple technical layers.

references.description: Sources, standards, specifications, literature, documentation, and supporting evidence.

title.description: Canonical title or name of the reference.

link.description: Optional URL, DOI, repository link, or other resolvable reference identifier.

description.description: Short explanation of the relevance or contribution of the reference.

observability.description: Ability to measure or observe reality.

constituentTechnicalElement.description: Any distinct element—artifact, agent, technique, standard, infrastructure component, competence, or institution—that is organized into the system.

productSpace.description: The structured set of all outputs—production technical objects, services, capabilities, data streams, or effects—that the system generates and delivers to its operational environment.

problemSpace.description: The bounded region of Technical Reality containing all Technical Problems, unmet Requirements, failure modes, and constraint tensions that the system is designed to intervene upon.

infrastructureType.description: Physical, informational, institutional, energetic, logistical, etc.

actType.description: Measurement, transformation, movement, control, inspection, etc.

principleType.description: Design, safety, reliability, optimization, security, etc.

gap.description: Difference between current and desired states.

temporalHorizon.description: Timeframe over which purpose is pursued.

inputOutputMapping.description: How inputs are transformed into outputs.

physicalLaw.description: Underlying physical or logical law governing the function.

abstractionLevel.description: Level of abstraction at which the function operates.

resourceType.description: Matter, energy, information, time, capital, labor.
